purpose: Hidden machine-readable plan for extracting textbook JPEG stills from the active canvas runtime; this block is not viewer-facing and does not participate in canonical ledger verification.

visibility_policy.reason_separate_from_ledger: The canonical ledger is hard-verified by strict deep equality; textbook extraction metadata must remain outside it.

capture_basis.capture_unit: seconds from loop start, before modulo reset
capture_basis.recommended_canvas_state: capture after drawing the animation frame at the requested timestamp
capture_basis.textbook_policy: Use the earliest clear picture; prefer visible action over symbolic explanation; avoid clutter; show what is earned before naming it.
capture_basis.important_caution: Camera reach changes during stage endings; avoid transition frames as primary textbook diagrams unless explaining viewport behavior.

carrier_family_resolution_note.description: The late runtime resolves into final catchment stations plus native carrier families. It is not pure lines only: the 73 unique resolved stations remain visible while construction scaffolding has faded away.
carrier_family_resolution_note.lawfulness_note: The visible carrier families are the native q=constant, r=constant, and q+r=constant families declared by the substrate, not a Cartesian square grid.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Sweep Becomes Boundary
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Origin centre visible with the first circle sweep underway; generated stations have not yet dominated the frame.
textbook_placement: Chapter 2 - A Sweep Becomes a Boundary
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: A fixed opening swept from a centre makes a boundary.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: It is the earliest clear action-frame for boundary generation.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: The circle is a swept construction boundary, not a coordinate object.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Boundary Stations Appear
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Six perimeter stations beginning to appear around the centre, with the sweep still readable.
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: Reached boundary positions can become construction stations.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Shows stationhood emerging from the boundary rather than from a grid.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: Stations are selected/generated substrate positions, not arbitrary particles.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Fixed Reach to Six Stations
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Centre, six generated stations, and radii from the centre becoming clear.
textbook_placement: Chapter 2 - A Sweep Becomes a Boundary
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: The same unit reach connects the centre to each generated neighbour.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Best compact frame for fixed unit reach before chords and cells make the image busier.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: Radii are measurement witnesses / overlays; the station rule remains axial.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Boundary Chords and Step Cycle
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Perimeter chords forming between adjacent generated stations; radii still visible.
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: Adjacent boundary stations are joined by fixed-opening steps.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Gives a clear still for boundary-to-boundary step and chord formation.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: Chords are joined construction marks; they are not the swept circumference itself.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Six Central Cells
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Six triangular cell fills around the centre, with radii and chords still readable.
textbook_placement: Chapter 7 - Six Triangles Around a Center
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: Six equal-opening cells fill the turn around the centre.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: The cell structure is visually strong but not yet fully fading.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: Cell fills are instructional overlays; the lawful points remain substrate stations.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Hexagon as Six Cells
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Circle, six radii, six perimeter chords, and six central triangular cells all readable.
textbook_placement: Chapter 8 - The Hexagon Inside the Construction
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: The hexagon is the outside edge of the six source-earned cells.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Strongest still for hexagon-as-constructed-body rather than imported polygon.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: The hexagon is a straight-edged reading of selected station joins, not the circumference.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Direction Families Emerging
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Construction scaffolding mostly fading while native carrier-family lines are becoming bright.
textbook_placement: Chapter 8 - The Hexagon Inside the Construction
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: The first construction exposes recurring direction families.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Shows direction arising after the hexagon/cell construction rather than before it.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: These are the three native triangular carrier families, not square-grid axes.

primary_snapshot_set.title: First Field Carriers
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Carrier families dominate the first-circle field with most construction marks gone.
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: Direction can be read before coordinates, slope, or equations.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: Direction has appeared; coordinates have not created it.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Field Extension by Generation
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Seven active centres with circle sweeps and generated perimeter stations appearing.
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: The same generation rule repeats from multiple active centres.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Shows field growth by repeated source rule rather than arbitrary decoration.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: Some generated coordinates may coincide; duplicates are identified by coordinate, not by brightness.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Triangular Tessellation Emerges
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Seven-centre construction with dense repeated triangular cells visible.
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: Repeated direction-family structure produces a triangular field of cells.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Good still for transition from first construction to field extension.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: The repeated cells are source-bound triangular cells, not an imported tiling background.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Medium Carrier Field
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Seven-field construction mostly released, with carrier families visible across a wider reach.
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: Carrier directions continue beyond the first boundary.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Good bridge image between the first-circle carrier still and final catchment still.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: Camera reach is wider here; mathematical unit relation is unchanged.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Full Construction Overlay Audit
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Thirteen-centre construction with circle sweeps, radii, chords, generated stations, and cells visible.
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: The final catchment is built by active centres and six-neighbour generation before resolving.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Best audit frame for all construction overlay types present at once.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: This is scaffolding, not the final substrate definition.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Resolved 73-Station Catchment
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Final resolved catchment stations with native carrier families bright; circles, joins, radii, chords, and cells gone or negligible.
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: The construction resolves into lawful field stations plus native carrier families.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Best canonical final-field still for the 73-station catchment.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: Final stations are the coordinate union of active centres and unique generated stations.

primary_snapshot_set.title: Direction-Family Dominant Final Frame
primary_snapshot_set.what_should_be_visible: Native carrier families visually dominate over the resolved station field.
textbook_placement: Chapter 23 - Lines, Slopes, and Direction Families
primary_snapshot_set.concept_supported: Direction families remain native to the triangular substrate and later coordinates only record them.
primary_snapshot_set.why_useful: Best aesthetic still for the moment that feels like families of direction lines.
primary_snapshot_set.caption_note: Not pure lines only: resolved stations remain part of the displayed final field.

optional_secondary_frames.caution: Too minimal for most textbook use, but useful as a seed image.

optional_secondary_frames.use: Camera reach transition after first circle
optional_secondary_frames.caution: Use only when explaining viewport behavior.
Useful if the book needs a clear active-centre expansion still before generation appears.
Best for appendix/audit rather than learner-facing early chapters.
Dense; needs captioning to prevent particle-like misreading.
Avoid as a concept diagram because the camera is transitioning.

frames_to_avoid_as_primary_diagrams.reason: First-circle to seven-circle camera transition; lawful but pedagogically ambiguous.
Seven-circle to thirteen-circle camera transition; reach is changing.
Final camera return toward loop reset; may make scale look mathematical.
Final resolved field is still transitional; use 52.0s instead.

caption_bank: The compass opening stays fixed; the camera may change reach.
Canvas projection is not the substrate; stations are integer axial (q,r) positions.
Circles, radii, chords, and cell fills are construction overlays, not substrate definitions.
Duplicate generated coordinates are identified as one station in the catchment.
Carrier families are q=constant, r=constant, and q+r=constant; they are not a square grid.
Coordinates and slopes come later as records of the field, not sources of it.
